patches.find: a 3.2× spread within just 20° of European latitude
patches.replace: a 3.2× spread across ~32° of European latitude (from the Canary Islands at 28.3°N through Scandinavia)
patches.rationale: The 3.2× range (0.642–2.075) requires the full north-south extent including the Canary Islands station (28.3°N) — not 20°. The honest range is ~32° (28°N through ~60°N).

patches.find: a 3.2× spread within 20° of European latitude
patches.replace: a 3.2× spread across ~32° of European latitude (Canary Islands at 28.3°N through Scandinavia)
patches.rationale: Same fix as detail_evidence: the full range requires Canary Islands, making the actual north-south span ~32°, not 20°.

patches.replace: recorded during Kp = 3–4 (unsettled conditions — NOAA G-scale geomagnetic disturbance begins at Kp 5 / G1)
patches.rationale: Kp 3–4 is 'unsettled' not 'disturbed' in NOAA terminology. G1 disturbance starts at Kp 5. The argument still holds: the dome's own Kp < 2 filter would reject this observation.

patches.replace: recorded at Kp = 3–4 (unsettled — NOAA G-scale disturbed conditions start at Kp 5)

patches.find: V51.0 introduces several new site pages: a "Live Power" convergence dashboard, a "Kill-Shot" binary test page, an "Audit Walkthrough" for AI models, and a "Tracking" page.
patches.replace: The current V51.x dome site presents several new pages: a "Live Power" convergence dashboard, a "Kill-Shot" binary test page, an "Audit Walkthrough" for AI models, and a "Tracking" page.
patches.rationale: The text said 'V51.0 introduces' implying this is a new feature in V51.0. The site is now V51.1+; these pages have been present across the V51 series. Removing the version-specific 'introduces' framing prevents the dome author from claiming we're reviewing an outdated version.
